title: North Carolina starter stats packet

precinctCentroids2020.caveat: VEST precinct records are loaded as centroid points in the current prototype.

countyCentroids2020.caveat: County centroids are coarse and mainly support broad orientation.

countyDerivedDistrictHeat2020.caveat: District heat fill is county-derived and not precinct-to-district aggregation.

planComparisons.name: 118th Congress enacted districts
caveats: This registry entry is a baseline enacted plan package, not an alternative proposal.
It proves the local plan-registry path before court, commission, and proposed maps are added.

planComparisons.name: NC 2022 court-ordered congressional plan
caveats: This is an official plan file from the NCGA 2022 redistricting process, normalized for Honeycombing's plan registry.
It is a plan overlay only; election and demographic comparisons still need separate aggregation.

planComparisons.name: NC 2023 enacted congressional plan
caveats: This is an official plan file from the NCGA 2023 redistricting process, normalized for Honeycombing's plan registry.

It provides a meaningfully different NC congressional-plan contrast against the 2022 court-ordered plan, but vote aggregation still uses centroid assignment until polygon apportionment is implemented.

starterSelections.name: Triangle
starterSelections.description: Wake, Durham, Orange, and nearby suburban growth corridors.
starterSelections.reviewerPrompt: Inspect how the court and enacted plans handle a high-growth Democratic urban/suburban cluster.

electionSignal.caveat: VEST precinct records are loaded as centroid points in the current prototype.

caveats: Bounds are rectangular starter selections for review triage, not official regions or legal communities of interest.
Population uses Census block internal points assigned to H3 cells; precinct signal uses VEST precinct centroids.
Plan touch counts use selected H3 cell centers inside plan polygons, not area or population apportionment.

starterSelections.name: Charlotte / Mecklenburg
starterSelections.description: Charlotte, Mecklenburg County, and the immediate suburban edge.
starterSelections.reviewerPrompt: Inspect whether the metro core and suburban edge are packed, cracked, or preserved across plans.

electionSignal.caveat: VEST precinct records are loaded as centroid points in the current prototype.

caveats: Bounds are rectangular starter selections for review triage, not official regions or legal communities of interest.
Population uses Census block internal points assigned to H3 cells; precinct signal uses VEST precinct centroids.
Plan touch counts use selected H3 cell centers inside plan polygons, not area or population apportionment.

starterSelections.name: Triad
starterSelections.description: Greensboro, Winston-Salem, High Point, and surrounding counties.
starterSelections.reviewerPrompt: Inspect the state's central urban/suburban corridor where multiple districts can touch compact clusters.

electionSignal.caveat: VEST precinct records are loaded as centroid points in the current prototype.

caveats: Bounds are rectangular starter selections for review triage, not official regions or legal communities of interest.
Population uses Census block internal points assigned to H3 cells; precinct signal uses VEST precinct centroids.
Plan touch counts use selected H3 cell centers inside plan polygons, not area or population apportionment.

starterSelections.name: Eastern Black Belt
starterSelections.description: Eastern North Carolina counties with substantial Black population and VRA-adjacent review questions.
starterSelections.reviewerPrompt: Inspect demographic concentration, plan continuity, and where Honeycombing must defer to CVAP/RPV evidence.

electionSignal.caveat: VEST precinct records are loaded as centroid points in the current prototype.

caveats: Bounds are rectangular starter selections for review triage, not official regions or legal communities of interest.
Population uses Census block internal points assigned to H3 cells; precinct signal uses VEST precinct centroids.
Plan touch counts use selected H3 cell centers inside plan polygons, not area or population apportionment.

starterSelections.name: Western North Carolina
starterSelections.description: Mountain and foothill region anchored by Asheville and western counties.
starterSelections.reviewerPrompt: Inspect rural/urban contrast, geography-driven boundaries, and whether the scaffold overstates natural mountain separations.

electionSignal.caveat: VEST precinct records are loaded as centroid points in the current prototype.

caveats: Bounds are rectangular starter selections for review triage, not official regions or legal communities of interest.
Population uses Census block internal points assigned to H3 cells; precinct signal uses VEST precinct centroids.
Plan touch counts use selected H3 cell centers inside plan polygons, not area or population apportionment.
Block and precinct assignments currently use internal points or centroids rather than polygon-to-H3 apportionment.
District heat fill is county-derived and should not be treated as precinct-to-district aggregation.
Plan coverage counts selected H3 cell centers inside plan polygons; it is a diagnostic coverage signal, not an area apportionment.
CVAP, racially polarized voting, candidate-of-choice, COI, ensemble, and formal VRA workflows are not included yet.
nextQuestions: Should the next evidence upgrade replace county-derived district heat with precinct-to-district aggregation?
Which NC regions should become named starter selections for expert review?
What COI source should be used first for a North Carolina overlay skeleton?
Which statistics should be considered mandatory before sharing with redistricting lawyers?
